put.summary: Ensure a dedicated LIST partition exists for `object_type`.
put.description:
Idempotent — safe to call multiple times. Existing rows in the default
partition are moved to the new partition atomically.

get.summary:
Check whether the current session user (or an explicit user) has a permission
on a resource. Uses a bundled session-validation + authz CTE for one DB round-trip
when checking the current session user.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Explicit subject to check as. Defaults to the current session user.

get.summary:
Expand a relation: return all subjects who hold the given relation on the object.
Resolves subject sets recursively.

List all objects of the given type that the current user (or an explicit user)
can access via the resolved roles for a permission. Includes both direct role
assignments and access granted via parent hierarchy.

post.summary: Write a single relation tuple. Idempotent — duplicate writes are silently ignored.

patch.summary: Batch write and/or delete relation tuples in a single transaction.

get.summary: Get the current authz schema. Returns null if authz is not enabled.

put.summary:
Replace the authz schema. Validates and compiles before persisting.
Setting schema to a valid document enables authz; this is the only way to enable it.

get.summary:
Explain why a permission check returned its result. Runs expand on all direct
role relations and reports which subjects appear, letting you trace a grant or
denial. Note: access granted purely via parent hierarchy is not reflected here.

404.description: Email not found or not verified
Email not found or already verified

302.description: Redirect to redirect_url with token and expires_at query params

parameters.name: redirect_url
parameters.description: URL to redirect to after authentication

302.description: Redirect to redirect_url with token and expires_at query params

409.description: Pending invite for this email already exists

404.description: No account with that email or no password identity

post.summary:
POST /v1/tokens — issue a short-lived JWT access token.
Requires `jwt_enabled = true` in app_config; returns 400 otherwise.

options.description: WebAuthn options object — pass directly to the browser's WebAuthn API.

state_token.description: Opaque state token; include in the corresponding finish request.

ErrorBody.description: Wire-format error body returned on all non-2xx responses.

ErrorResponse.description: Top-level error envelope for all API error responses.

parent_relation.description: The relation name on this resource that points to parent objects.

parent_resource.description: The resource type of the parent.

token.description: Plaintext token — only present on creation, never returned again.
One-time token to exchange via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=magic_link`.
One-time token to use in the corresponding session grant.
One-time token to exchange via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=password_reset`.
Opaque bearer token — store securely, transmit as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

current.description: Whether this is the caller's current session.

StepUpResponse.description: Returned when the user has TOTP enrolled — caller must complete the step-up flow.

step_up_token.description: Short-lived signed token to present when completing the step-up.
